title: Fragment: 'I Stood Upon A Heaven-Cleaving Turret'.
content:
I stood upon a heaven-cleaving turret
    Which overlooked a wide Metropolis -
    And in the temple of my heart my Spirit
    Lay prostrate, and with parted lips did kiss
    The dust of Desolations [altar] hearth -
    And with a voice too faint to falter
    It shook that trembling fane with its weak prayer
    'Twas noon, - the sleeping skies were blue
    The city
meaning:
This evocative piece by Percy Bysshe Shelley, titled "Fragment: 'I Stood Upon A Heaven-Cleaving Turret'.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
